# DGE Integration Guide

## If your project has a development workflow

DGE is self-contained within the `dge/` folder.
It does not directly modify your project's `docs/` or any existing files.

## How to incorporate DGE output into your existing workflow

1. Run a DGE session -> Discover Gaps
2. "Implement" -> A Spec is generated in `dge/specs/` (status: draft)
3. Review the Spec (update status to: reviewed)
4. Transcribe the reviewed Spec into your official `docs/` following your workflow
5. After transcription, change the status of the file in `dge/specs/` to `migrated` and fill in the `migrated_to` field with the canonical document path

## Mapping to your existing workflow phases

| Your phase | DGE action |
|---|---|
| PLAN / DESIGN | Run a DGE session. Discover Gaps -> Generate Specs |
| IMPLEMENT | Implement based on reviewed Specs in `dge/specs/` |
| VERIFY | Confirm that all DGE Gaps have been addressed |

## Source of Truth rule

**If a DGE Spec and existing docs conflict, the existing docs are the Source of Truth.**

DGE Specs are "proposals" and are not the official specifications of the project.
If an API definition generated by DGE differs from your `api-surface.md`, then `api-surface.md` is correct.

## What DGE touches / does not touch

| Touches | Does not touch |
|---------|----------------|
| `dge/sessions/` | `docs/` |
| `dge/specs/` | `.claude/rules/` |
| `dge/custom/` | Existing spec files |
| `.claude/skills/dge-*.md` | CLAUDE.md (initial proposal only) |

## Spec lifecycle

```
draft       Auto-generated by DGE. Not yet reviewed.
            -> Do not implement. Review is required.

reviewed    Reviewed by a human. Ready for implementation.
            -> Implement according to your workflow.

migrated    Transcribed to the project's official docs/.
            -> This file is for reference only. See migrated_to for the canonical document.
```

## For projects without a workflow

You can use reviewed Specs in `dge/specs/` directly as the basis for implementation.
Transcription to official `docs/` is not required.
